info.title: Headway API
info.description: Serves computed transit metrics with full lineage, the DQ resolution workflow, and the audited certification action. This API never computes a reported figure; it serves what the calculation library produced, joined to its provenance. Reported values are JSON strings (exact NUMERIC, never float). Auth: local accounts (ADR-0011); the native OIDC relying party is the next increment behind the same {sub, username, role} claim set.

parameters.name: category

schema.description: Filter on the honesty boundary (migration 0024): 'ntd' for regulatory-pipeline figures, 'ops' for operations metrics (on-time performance, headway adherence — never certifiable, never NTD-reported). Every row carries its category either way, so the UI can badge operations figures.
schema.title: Category

parameters.description: Filter on the honesty boundary (migration 0024): 'ntd' for regulatory-pipeline figures, 'ops' for operations metrics (on-time performance, headway adherence — never certifiable, never NTD-reported). Every row carries its category either way, so the UI can badge operations figures.

schema.title: Response List Metric Values Metrics Values Get

get.description:
Download /metrics/values as CSV or XLSX (handoff 0017, design point
5): ONE row assembly feeds both formats, so the XLSX values are
byte-equal to the CSV values cell for cell (pinned by test).

200.description: The same rows GET /metrics/values serves, as a CSV or XLSX download. Figures are the exact served strings (XLSX cells are TEXT — never spreadsheet numbers); the preview disclaimer (and a simulated-data warning when any row is simulated) leads the CSV and forms the XLSX's first sheet.

get.description:
Compare one metric across 2–4 comparands (calc versions of the same
figure, or periods) per scope. Serves the calc library's persisted rows
VERBATIM (composition of the same reader as GET /metrics/values — this
endpoint never computes a figure) plus exact-decimal deltas as
comparison affordances; direction metadata comes from the calc
library's metric registry (coverage only today).

parameters.name: comparand

schema.description: 2–4 comparison columns; repeat the parameter. Each comparand is '<period_start>..<period_end>' (ISO dates, the half-open period exactly as computed) optionally followed by '@<calc_name>:<calc_version>' to pin one calculation version — for example '2026-07-01..2026-08-01' or '2026-07-14..2026-07-16@dr_vrm_v0:0.1.1'. The first comparand is the baseline.
schema.title: Comparand

parameters.description: 2–4 comparison columns; repeat the parameter. Each comparand is '<period_start>..<period_end>' (ISO dates, the half-open period exactly as computed) optionally followed by '@<calc_name>:<calc_version>' to pin one calculation version — for example '2026-07-01..2026-08-01' or '2026-07-14..2026-07-16@dr_vrm_v0:0.1.1'. The first comparand is the baseline.

parameters.name: scope

schema.description: Row scopes (repeat the parameter), e.g. 'agency' and 'mode:bus'. Omit to include every scope that has at least one figure in any comparand ('agency' first, the rest sorted).
schema.title: Scope

parameters.description: Row scopes (repeat the parameter), e.g. 'agency' and 'mode:bus'. Omit to include every scope that has at least one figure in any comparand ('agency' first, the rest sorted).

get.description:
"Explain this number": the full provenance tree from the reported
figure down to the raw records that produced it (ADR-0007).

Dual credential (handoff 0006 follow-up): a signed-in human session OR a
``read:metrics`` machine key. The machine path is rate-limited per key
and audited with actor ``key:<key_prefix>``; the human path is unchanged.

The fixed statements the signing ceremony renders: the ESIGN-style
intent statement the typed name/title are entered against, and the
honest-scope statement printed on the certificate.

Every certification record, oldest first (any signed-in role — the
record view; the public surface serves fingerprints only).

The certificate view: the signature block (typed name/title,
fingerprint), everything the signature covers (the parsed document),
the raw signed bytes, and a live verification result.

Re-verify the stored certificate against its stored signature (any
signed-in role). The same check, without login, is
GET /public/certifications/{id}/verify — it serves no certifier
identity, only the verdict and fingerprint.

post.description:
Record a statistician's approval (append-only, audited).

From the next calc run onward, a >2% missing-data share on a covered
(metric, scope, period) factors up under this attestation instead of
refusing — and the factored figure carries the attestation's provenance
permanently.

get.description:
List attestations (any signed-in role — the record is the point).
Revoked rows are served by default: revocation is history, not
deletion; filter with include_revoked=false for the live set.

post.description:
Revoke an attestation (never delete it — migration-0029 trigger).

Figures already factored under it keep their attestation provenance
forever (the history is honest); revocation stops FUTURE calc runs from
factoring under it. Audited in the same transaction.

schema.title: Response List Issues Dq Issues Get

get.description:
Severity/status counts over the same rows (and the same optional
status filter) as GET /dq/issues — composition of the one issues query,
counted in the open, no new tables (handoff 0017).

post.description:
Close ONE p. 146 refusal issue under a recorded statistician
attestation — the explicit 'attested' state (handoff 0019, never a
generic 'resolved', never a deletion).

Refuses loudly when: the issue does not exist (404); the issue is not
the p. 146 refusal type (409 — no other gap has a statistician cure:
'agencies must not collect a smaller sample than the chosen sampling
plan prescribes', 2026 NTD Policy Manual p. 149); the issue is already
closed (409); the attestation does not exist (404) or is revoked (409).
The resolution text is built here from the attestation record. Audited
in the same transaction.

schema.title: Response List Keys Machine Keys Get

get.description:
Computed values for machine consumers (scope ``read:metrics``): same
filters and shape as the human ``GET /metrics/values``. Each row's
lineage is available from ``GET /metrics/values/{metric_value_id}/
lineage`` with this same key.

parameters.name: category

schema.description: Filter on the honesty boundary (migration 0024): 'ntd' regulatory-pipeline figures or 'ops' operations metrics (never certifiable, never NTD-reported).
schema.title: Category

parameters.description: Filter on the honesty boundary (migration 0024): 'ntd' regulatory-pipeline figures or 'ops' operations metrics (never certifiable, never NTD-reported).

schema.title: Response Machine List Metric Values Machine Metrics Get

get.description:
Every declared service-day override (optionally bounded to
[from_date, to_date), half-open — the calc period convention), with its
reason and audit attribution. Any signed-in role may read — the
calendar policy must be visible to the people it governs.

schema.title: Response List Service Day Overrides Settings Service Days Get

put.description:
Declare (or replace) one service day's override — a holiday
schedule reassignment and/or an atypical-day flag, with a required
reason (certifying official only). The old and new declarations land
together in the audit trail; figures already computed under the old
declaration are untouched (each carries its governing declaration in
its own detail).

delete.description:
Remove one service day's override (certifying official only): the
date returns to its day-of-week schedule type, typical. Removing a
declaration is a policy change too — the removed declaration lands in
the audit trail, and figures computed under it keep their snapshot.

get.description:
Every agency policy setting, with its plain-language description and
who last changed it. Any signed-in role may read — policy must be
visible to the people it governs.

put.description:
Change one policy setting (certifying official only). The old and new
values land together in the audit trail; the change and its audit event
commit in one transaction.

post.description:
Authenticated push of one demand_response_trips CSV file (handoff
0013): the demand_response_trip v0 wire contract as the raw request
body. Same connector discipline as the TIDES path — content-addressed,
store-before-produce, envelope source = the key's bound source_label
(a simulator key is bound to 'dr_simulated'; a client-supplied source is
ignored).

get.description: Serve the MR-20 preview package (NOT reportable) for one month.

parameters.name: month

schema.description: Calendar month as YYYY-MM (e.g. 2026-07), UTC, half-open period.
schema.title: Month

parameters.description: Calendar month as YYYY-MM (e.g. 2026-07), UTC, half-open period.

200.description: The headway_calc.mr20 package for the month, verbatim: form/generator/period header, reportable=false + banner, programmatically enumerated caveats, and the four MR-20 data points per mode plus fleet totals, each cell carrying full provenance or an explicit null + reason.

get.description:
Download the MR-20 preview package as CSV/XLSX (handoff 0017, design
point 5). Same package build as GET /reports/mr20; one row assembly
feeds both formats (XLSX values byte-equal to CSV values, pinned by
test); XLSX cells are TEXT so figures survive exactly.

200.description: The MR-20 preview package as a CSV or XLSX download: one row per (scope, metric) cell, values VERBATIM from the package; the NOT-REPORTABLE banner and every enumerated caveat lead the CSV and form the XLSX's first sheet.

get.description:
Download the monthly agency workbook (handoff 0020). One assembly
feeds both formats — XLSX cell values byte-equal to the CSV variant
(pinned by test); the CSV introduces each sheet with a '## <title>'
marker line. This endpoint queries and formats; it never computes a
figure (year-over-year deltas deliberately excluded — see
GET /metrics/compare).

200.description: The monthly agency workbook (handoff 0020) as an XLSX or CSV download — Headway's OWN generic layout: a 'Read first' banner sheet (NOT-REPORTABLE, honest-scope absences, certification block when figures are certified), a Ridership-by-mode sheet (UPT month totals, average weekday/Saturday/Sunday UPT with typical/atypical splits, Days Operated per schedule type) and an Operations sheet (VOMS plus migration-0024-badged OTP/headway-adherence). Every data cell is the verbatim served figure with a VISIBLE provenance column (metric_value_id); absent cells are stated, never invented or zero-filled; year-over-year columns show the prior year month's figure verbatim with its own provenance.

get.description:
Download the S&S-50 preview package (headway_calc.ss50, served
verbatim into a grid) as CSV/XLSX — handoff 0017, design point 5.

200.description: The S&S-50 non-major monthly summary package as a CSV or XLSX download: one row per (mode, type-of-service) cell including explicit zero rows; the NOT-REPORTABLE banner, citations, caveats and the excluded-event accounting lead the CSV and form the XLSX's first sheet.

schema.title: Response List Certified Values Public Metrics Certified Get

get.description:
Public tamper-evidence check (handoff 0019, design point 6/7):
re-verifies the stored certificate bytes against the stored Ed25519
signature, server-side, and returns the verdict.

PUBLIC-SAFE by construction: the response carries the verdict,
algorithm, fingerprint, and timestamp — never the certifier's identity,
never the document, never any auth material. Rate-limited per client IP
exactly like /public/metrics/certified (which serves the
certification_id + fingerprint this endpoint verifies).

Serve the agency logo. UNAUTHENTICATED by design — the app shell and
public pages show it before sign-in; the only per-caller control is the
same per-IP token bucket as the public open-data endpoint. The logo is
the least sensitive object in the system: a public image the agency
chose to publish.

200.description: The agency logo bytes, with its stored content type.

404.description: No logo has been uploaded yet.

post.description:
Upload the agency logo (certifying official only — the same role that
owns every other branding setting). SVG or PNG, at most 512 KiB. The
bytes go to the object store BEFORE the settings row and audit event
commit together — the store-before-record ordering of ingest.

get.description:
The branding bundle for the app shell: display name, the two brand
colors (contrast-guaranteed at write time), and whether a logo exists.
UNAUTHENTICATED by design, per-IP rate limited — the shell brands itself
before sign-in, and nothing here is sensitive.

post.description:
Enter one safety event; the classifier runs synchronously and the
entry, its classification, and the audit record commit together.

schema.title: Response List Events Safety Events Get

get.description:
Classification counts over the same rows (and the same month/mode
filters) as GET /safety/events — composition of the one events query,
counted in the open, no new tables (handoff 0017).

post.description:
Append-only correction: enter the CORRECTED event in full; the
original stays untouched except for its superseded_by link.

parameters.name: month

schema.description: Month for the S&S-50 deadline rows (YYYY-MM). Defaults to the current UTC month. S&S-40 deadlines are month-independent (every open major event is listed).
schema.title: Month

parameters.description: Month for the S&S-50 deadline rows (YYYY-MM). Defaults to the current UTC month. S&S-40 deadlines are month-independent (every open major event is listed).

get.description:
The plan wizard's vocabulary, straight from the calc selector's
constants (Table 41.01 / §41.07), with the eligibility guidance.

Look up one ready-to-use table cell (verbatim, cited) without
creating a plan — the wizard's live preview. All encoded cells are
readable here, including the reference-only grouped-APTL cells.

post.description:
Create a sampling plan. The required sizes are the calc selector's
verbatim table cells; the selector version is recorded on the row.

schema.title: Response List Plans Sampling Plans Get
Response List Draws Sampling Plans  Plan Id  Draws Get

post.description:
One random-selection act for one period: seeded, recorded, WITHOUT
replacement (§63.03), drawn by the versioned calc drawer.

Record one ride-check observation for one drawn unit. Append-only:
corrections supersede.

schema.title: Response List Measurements Sampling Plans  Plan Id  Measurements Get

post.description:
Append-only correction: enter the CORRECTED observation in full; the
original stays untouched except for its superseded_by link (the
migration-0017 supersede pattern; migration 0020's trigger enforces
it).

get.description:
Measured vs required, per draw and overall, with the worksheet of
drawn-but-unmeasured units.

Download the ride-check worksheet (handoff 0017, design point 5):
the same plan/draw/measurement records GET /sampling/plans/{id}/progress
summarizes, one row per selected unit. One row assembly feeds both
formats (XLSX values byte-equal to CSV values, pinned by test).

200.description: The plan's measurement worksheet as a CSV or XLSX download: one row per selected unit per draw with its measured state; the plan's requirement, the undersampled/estimate-ready state and the §63.11 retention note lead the CSV and form the XLSX's first sheet.

404.description: No sampling plan with that id exists.

post.description:
The §83 APTL estimate over the plan's active measurements. REFUSES
Base-option plans and undersampled plans; the result is a SAMPLED
ESTIMATE (provenance-labeled) and is never written to
computed.metric_values.

Preview the figure impact of proposed policy-knob values for one
period — read-only, ephemeral, never certifiable (module docstring).
Baseline = the CURRENT audited settings (app.settings, the same
precedence a real run uses); proposed = the same computation with only
the proposed knobs overridden.

AttestRequest.title: AttestRequest
AttestRequest.description:
Close a p. 146 refusal issue under a recorded statistician
attestation (handoff 0019). The resolution text is built server-side
from the attestation — the caller supplies only the reference.

statistician_credentials.title: Statistician Credentials
statistician_credentials.description: A plain-language summary of why this statistician is qualified (the manual prescribes no specific qualifications; the agency is accountable for them).

method_description.title: Method Description
method_description.description: The approved factoring method, in the statistician's terms.

document_reference.title: Document Reference
document_reference.description: External pointer to the approval document (file path, document system id). The document itself is never stored here.

scope_pattern.title: Scope Pattern
scope_pattern.description: Which figure scopes the approval covers, as an fnmatch pattern over computed.metric_values.scope: 'agency', 'mode:bus', 'mode:DR:tos:*', or '*' for every scope.

chrome_note.title: Chrome Note
chrome_note.default: The chrome theme carries one color set, validated for readability against itself. A theme is applied only where it renders readably; in a display mode it was not validated for (dark mode), the shell keeps the neutral Headway chrome and says so — stated, never silent.

BrandingResponse.title: BrandingResponse
BrandingResponse.description:
What the app shell needs to brand itself. Colors here have already
passed the WCAG AA contrast guardrail at write time.

CertificationCertificate.title: CertificationCertificate
CertificationCertificate.description:
The full certificate view (the frontend's certificate screen):
the stored canonical document (raw signed text + parsed object), the
signature, and a live verification result.

CertificationRecord.title: CertificationRecord
CertificationRecord.description:
One certification as listed/viewed. Legacy (pre-signature) records
carry signed=false and NULL signature fields — honest history.

ChromeTheme.title: ChromeTheme
ChromeTheme.description:
Themed nav chrome (branding v2, handoff 0017): the pairs here have
already passed the server-side WCAG AA pair guardrail at write time
(header_fg on header_bg >= 4.5:1, accent on header_bg >= 4.5:1).

Comparand.title: Comparand
Comparand.description: One comparison column, echoed back exactly as parsed.

CompareCell.title: CompareCell
CompareCell.description:
One (scope, comparand) cell: the FULL metric-value row (verbatim —
the receipt affordance needs metric_value_id, badges need detail/
category/certification_status) or an explicit missing reason, plus the
exact deltas.

source_label.title: Source Label
source_label.description: The envelope source this key is bound to (required for ingest keys); e.g. 'tides_simulated' for a simulator key.

DqIssueCounts.title: DqIssueCounts
DqIssueCounts.description:
Counts for the /dq summary cards (handoff 0017, design point 2):
counted over EXACTLY the rows GET /dq/issues serves under the same
filter, so a card total can never disagree with the table below it.
Missing severities/statuses appear as explicit zeros.

period_label.title: Period Label
period_label.description: Which period this draw covers at the plan's frequency (for example 2026-Q1, 2026-01, or 2026-W14). One draw per period.

service_units.title: Service Units
service_units.description: ALL service units you expect to operate in this period, in the plan's unit of sampling (§63.07) — for scheduled service including trippers, shuttles, and other special operations. Use period-qualified ids (for example 2026-01-15/trip-5012) so a unit id never repeats across periods.

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for the draw. Leave blank to let Headway generate one from a cryptographic randomness source. Either way the seed is RECORDED on the draw so the selection can be reproduced and audited — along with seed_source ('generated' or 'client'), because Headway can only vouch for the randomness of a seed it generated itself.

oversample_units.title: Oversample Units
oversample_units.description: Extra units to select BEYOND the plan's required per-period size. Oversampling is allowed only when the extra units are selected randomly — Headway draws them from the same seeded random order, and flags them on the draw record.

annual_upt_100pct.title: Annual Upt 100Pct
annual_upt_100pct.description: Your agency's 100% count of annual UPT for this mode and type of service — the §83.01(a) expansion factor, as a decimal string.

upt_100pct_by_day_type.title: Upt 100Pct By Day Type
upt_100pct_by_day_type.description: Optional: 100% UPT counts by type of service day (Weekday / Saturday / Sunday) to also get per-day-type estimates (§83.01(b)). Every measurement must then carry its service-day type.

KeyListItem.title: KeyListItem
KeyListItem.description: Listing shape: prefix + metadata only — never the hash, never the key.

LineageNode.title: LineageNode
LineageNode.description:
One node of the provenance tree.

``transform_name``/``transform_version`` describe the transform that
PRODUCED this node from its ``inputs`` (per lineage.edges). Raw records
are leaves: no transform, no inputs.

unit_id.title: Unit Id
unit_id.description: The drawn service unit this ride check observed.

observed_upt.title: Observed Upt
observed_upt.description: Unlinked passenger trips (boardings) counted on this unit by the ride checker.

observed_pmt.title: Observed Pmt
observed_pmt.description: Passenger miles traveled measured on this unit, as a decimal string (values are exact decimals, never floats).

service_day_type.title: Service Day Type
service_day_type.description: Weekday, Saturday, or Sunday — required later only if you want estimates broken out by type of service day.

service_date.title: Service Date
service_date.description: The date the ride check was performed.

unit_id.title: Unit Id
unit_id.description: The drawn service unit this ride check observed.

observed_upt.title: Observed Upt
observed_upt.description: Unlinked passenger trips (boardings) counted on this unit by the ride checker.

observed_pmt.title: Observed Pmt
observed_pmt.description: Passenger miles traveled measured on this unit, as a decimal string (values are exact decimals, never floats).

service_day_type.title: Service Day Type
service_day_type.description: Weekday, Saturday, or Sunday — required later only if you want estimates broken out by type of service day.

service_date.title: Service Date
service_date.description: The date the ride check was performed.

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the original observation is being corrected (kept in the audit log — the original measurement itself is never edited).

report_year.title: Report Year
report_year.description: The NTD report year this plan samples for.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: NTD mode code the ready-to-use plans cover: DR (demand response), VP (commuter vanpool), MB/TB (bus), CR (commuter rail), LR/HR/MR/AG (other rail).

type_of_service.title: Type Of Service
type_of_service.description: Type of service this plan samples (e.g. DO for directly operated, PT for purchased transportation). Sampling meets the FTA floor per mode AND type of service.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Unit of sampling and measurement (Table 41.01): vehicle_days, one_way_trips, round_trips, one_way_car_trips, or one_way_train_trips — must match the mode.

efficiency_option.title: Efficiency Option
efficiency_option.description: 'aptl' (report a 100% UPT count, sample average passenger trip length; for bus this is the Without Route Grouping column) or 'base' (sample both UPT and PMT; note that Base-option ESTIMATION is not yet available — Section 70).

frequency.title: Frequency
frequency.description: Sampling frequency: quarterly, monthly, or weekly.

PreviewSide.title: PreviewSide
PreviewSide.description:
One variant's outcome for one metric: the would-be value (or an
honest refusal) plus every would-be finding. Nothing here was written
anywhere.

PublicCertificationRef.title: PublicCertificationRef
PublicCertificationRef.description:
The public face of a certification (handoff 0019, design point 7):
WHICH record certified the figure, WHEN, and the signing key's
fingerprint — enough to verify tamper-evidence via
GET /public/certifications/{id}/verify. NO certifier identity here
(that lives in the authenticated record); a NULL fingerprint is a
pre-signature legacy certification, honestly unsigned.

PublicMetricValue.title: PublicMetricValue
PublicMetricValue.description: A certified figure, publishable form. No PII, no certifier identity.

SafetyEventCounts.title: SafetyEventCounts
SafetyEventCounts.description:
Counts for the /safety summary cards (handoff 0017, design point 2):
counted over EXACTLY the rows GET /safety/events serves under the same
filters, so a card can never disagree with the table. Classification is
each event's LATEST verdict; an event with no classification row counts
as 'unclassified' (never guessed); superseded events are counted
separately AND inside their classification bucket, exactly as the list
shows them.

occurred_at.title: Occurred At
occurred_at.description: When the event happened (include the time zone).

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: The ONE mode this event is reported in. If more than one mode was involved, apply the Predominant Use Rule (p. 15): rail wins over non-rail; otherwise pick the mode carrying more passengers.

type_of_service.title: Type Of Service
type_of_service.description: Type of service (e.g. DO for directly operated, PT for purchased transportation).

narrative.title: Narrative
narrative.description: What happened, in your own words.

fatalities.title: Fatalities
fatalities.description: How many people died (confirmed within 30 days; suicides are included — Exhibit 5, p. 16). Do NOT count deaths from illness, overdose, or natural causes (p. 20); DO count a death of undetermined cause in a rail right-of-way that may be the result of collision or electrocution (p. 20).

injuries.title: Injuries
injuries.description: How many people were taken directly from the scene for medical care (Exhibit 5, p. 16 — immediate transport, not later self-referral). Do NOT count transport solely for illness, natural causes, exposure, intoxication, overdose, or an unrelated mental-health evaluation, or declarations of self-harm with no evident injury (p. 22); DO count an injury the event caused, e.g. a passenger's heart attack caused by a collision (p. 22).

property_damage_usd.title: Property Damage Usd
property_damage_usd.description: Estimated property damage in dollars. Sum the damage to ALL vehicles and property involved or affected — transit and non-transit — plus the cost of clearing wreckage (p. 25; e.g. a $15,000 car plus $12,000 bus damage is reported as $27,000, Example 7A). Leave blank if not yet assessed — Headway never treats unknown damage as $0.

serious_injury.title: Serious Injury
serious_injury.description: Rail only: did anyone suffer a serious injury (p. 21) — hospitalization for more than 48 hours within 7 days; a fracture of any bone (except simple fractures of fingers, toes, or nose); severe hemorrhages, or nerve, muscle, or tendon damage; an internal organ; or 2nd/3rd-degree burns or burns over 5% of the body surface? Answer yes even if the person was NOT transported from the scene — serious injuries 'may or may not have been transported' (p. 21).

substantial_damage.title: Substantial Damage
substantial_damage.description: Rail: did the damage disrupt operations AND require towing, rescue (e.g. a rescue train), on-site maintenance, or immediate removal before safe operation (p. 25)? Do NOT count cracked windows; dents, bends, or small punctures; broken lights or mirrors; or removal under the vehicle's own power for minor repair, testing, or recorder download (p. 25). For cyber events: did the intrusion disrupt operations (Scenario G, p. 19)?

towed.title: Towed
towed.description: Was any vehicle (transit or not) towed away from the scene? For a non-rail collision involving a transit revenue vehicle this alone makes the event reportable (p. 17); for a rail collision, a tow-away IS substantial damage (Example 7C, p. 27).

evacuation_life_safety.title: Evacuation Life Safety
evacuation_life_safety.description: Were people evacuated from a transit facility or vehicle for life-safety reasons (p. 17)?

assault_on_worker.title: Assault On Worker
assault_on_worker.description: Was a transit worker assaulted? An injury is NOT required for this to be reportable on the S&S-50 (p. 3).

grade_crossing.title: Grade Crossing
grade_crossing.description: Rail: did the collision occur at a rail grade crossing or intersection (p. 17)?

runaway_train.title: Runaway Train
runaway_train.description: Rail: did a revenue vehicle move uncommanded, uncontrolled, or unmanned — operator incapacitated, sleeping, or absent, or an electrical, mechanical, or software failure — on the mainline, in a yard, or in a shop (p. 17)?

evacuation_to_rail_row.title: Evacuation To Rail Row
evacuation_to_rail_row.description: Rail: did people evacuate to the controlled rail right-of-way (transit-directed or self-evacuation)? Evacuation to a platform does not count unless it was for life safety (p. 17).

SafetyEventCreate.title: SafetyEventCreate
SafetyEventCreate.description:
One safety event as entered by agency staff. Field descriptions are
plain language — the entry form asks questions, not jargon.

period_convention.title: Period Convention
period_convention.description: How the month filter buckets events: half-open [period_start, period_end), UTC, on occurred_at. Months are bucketed by when the event occurred in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), not local time: an event late on the last evening of a month local time may fall into the next month's bucket. Enter event times with their time zone and Headway places them consistently.

SubscriptionResponse.title: SubscriptionResponse
SubscriptionResponse.description: Subscription shape served back — deliberately secret-free.

occurred_at.title: Occurred At
occurred_at.description: When the event happened (include the time zone).

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: The ONE mode this event is reported in. If more than one mode was involved, apply the Predominant Use Rule (p. 15): rail wins over non-rail; otherwise pick the mode carrying more passengers.

type_of_service.title: Type Of Service
type_of_service.description: Type of service (e.g. DO for directly operated, PT for purchased transportation).

narrative.title: Narrative
narrative.description: What happened, in your own words.

fatalities.title: Fatalities
fatalities.description: How many people died (confirmed within 30 days; suicides are included — Exhibit 5, p. 16). Do NOT count deaths from illness, overdose, or natural causes (p. 20); DO count a death of undetermined cause in a rail right-of-way that may be the result of collision or electrocution (p. 20).

injuries.title: Injuries
injuries.description: How many people were taken directly from the scene for medical care (Exhibit 5, p. 16 — immediate transport, not later self-referral). Do NOT count transport solely for illness, natural causes, exposure, intoxication, overdose, or an unrelated mental-health evaluation, or declarations of self-harm with no evident injury (p. 22); DO count an injury the event caused, e.g. a passenger's heart attack caused by a collision (p. 22).

property_damage_usd.title: Property Damage Usd
property_damage_usd.description: Estimated property damage in dollars. Sum the damage to ALL vehicles and property involved or affected — transit and non-transit — plus the cost of clearing wreckage (p. 25; e.g. a $15,000 car plus $12,000 bus damage is reported as $27,000, Example 7A). Leave blank if not yet assessed — Headway never treats unknown damage as $0.

serious_injury.title: Serious Injury
serious_injury.description: Rail only: did anyone suffer a serious injury (p. 21) — hospitalization for more than 48 hours within 7 days; a fracture of any bone (except simple fractures of fingers, toes, or nose); severe hemorrhages, or nerve, muscle, or tendon damage; an internal organ; or 2nd/3rd-degree burns or burns over 5% of the body surface? Answer yes even if the person was NOT transported from the scene — serious injuries 'may or may not have been transported' (p. 21).

substantial_damage.title: Substantial Damage
substantial_damage.description: Rail: did the damage disrupt operations AND require towing, rescue (e.g. a rescue train), on-site maintenance, or immediate removal before safe operation (p. 25)? Do NOT count cracked windows; dents, bends, or small punctures; broken lights or mirrors; or removal under the vehicle's own power for minor repair, testing, or recorder download (p. 25). For cyber events: did the intrusion disrupt operations (Scenario G, p. 19)?

towed.title: Towed
towed.description: Was any vehicle (transit or not) towed away from the scene? For a non-rail collision involving a transit revenue vehicle this alone makes the event reportable (p. 17); for a rail collision, a tow-away IS substantial damage (Example 7C, p. 27).

evacuation_life_safety.title: Evacuation Life Safety
evacuation_life_safety.description: Were people evacuated from a transit facility or vehicle for life-safety reasons (p. 17)?

assault_on_worker.title: Assault On Worker
assault_on_worker.description: Was a transit worker assaulted? An injury is NOT required for this to be reportable on the S&S-50 (p. 3).

grade_crossing.title: Grade Crossing
grade_crossing.description: Rail: did the collision occur at a rail grade crossing or intersection (p. 17)?

runaway_train.title: Runaway Train
runaway_train.description: Rail: did a revenue vehicle move uncommanded, uncontrolled, or unmanned — operator incapacitated, sleeping, or absent, or an electrical, mechanical, or software failure — on the mainline, in a yard, or in a shop (p. 17)?

evacuation_to_rail_row.title: Evacuation To Rail Row
evacuation_to_rail_row.description: Rail: did people evacuate to the controlled rail right-of-way (transit-directed or self-evacuation)? Evacuation to a platform does not count unless it was for life safety (p. 17).

reason.title: Reason
reason.description: Why the original entry is being corrected (kept in the audit log — the original event itself is never edited).
